title: Pteracanthus lamium
taxonomy: Pteracanthus lamium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other members of the Lamiaceae family, such as its square stem and opposite leaf arrangement. The genus Pteracanthus is a small one, containing only a few species of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. As a member of the Lamiaceae family, Pteracanthus lamium is further classified as a herbaceous perennial plant. Its classification as a member of the mint family reflects its similarities in leaf and stem morphology to other plants in this group.
